text: Wind River Met Society monthly meeting is moved online this month due to weather. Link is in the pinned post.
Winter Storm Warning issued for the western Bighorn Basin. 6 to 12 inches expected above 6000 feet through tomorrow night.
Park County road crews are out. Avoid travel on WY-120 north of Cody until further notice.
Greybull wind gust to 52 mph down at the river. Power flickering in town.
Worland clear but bitter. Minus 8 at sunrise and dropping.
Black ice on the Wind River Canyon road. Two cars already off near the dam.
Reminder: report verified conditions only. Include location and time of observation.
Hazardous Weather Outlook: NWS Riverton has the Bighorn Basin under a Winter Weather Advisory through tomorrow evening. Spotters check in.
Snow rates picking up over Sylvan Pass and the South Fork. 1 to 2 inches per hour possible at elevation.
Cody airport visibility down to half a mile, wind out of the west gusting near 40.
Powell holding at light snow, but 14A is getting slick.
Heavy snow on the bench south of Cody, drifting across County Road 6WX. Cattle bunched in the lee of the ridge.
Meeteetse reporting a ground blizzard. Near zero visibility on the Greybull highway.
Cold front moving through tonight. Strong winds and blowing snow likely across the Basin. Report as conditions change.
First measurable snow of the season in Cody. Big flakes coming down steady.
Clark getting hammered. Three inches in the last hour and still going.
Lovell calm for now but temps dropping fast. Wind picking up out of the northwest.
Wind River front range socked in. Chains advised over Togwotee Pass.
